subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Screens & Cards
- **2** Screens
- **48** Code Cards total
- **24** black Code Cards
- **24** white Code Cards
- **110** Keyword Cards
- **440** total words across Keyword Cards

#### Tokens & Timer
- **4** Interception Tokens (white)
- **4** Miscommunication Tokens (black)
- **1** Sand Timer (30 seconds)

#### Sheets & Rulebook
- **50** Note Sheets
- **1** Rulebook

#### Keyword Card Handling
- **No pre-reading** — Do not look at keyword faces before setup.
- **No pre-shuffling** — Follow rulebook guidance to avoid repeated words across sessions.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Step 0 — Player-Count Audit Reference
- **Core format** — Two teams play against each other.
- **Team size range** — Rulebook states teams are 2 to 4 players each.
- **3-player mode** — Rulebook states dedicated 3-player rules are at the end of the rulebook.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Mode change** — Use the dedicated 3-player rules at the end of the rulebook.

--- 4 to 8 Players ---
- **Team split** — Split into two teams as evenly as possible.
- **Seat layout** — Teammates sit on one side; opponents sit opposite.

#### Team Setup
- **Screen** — Each team takes 1 Screen.
- **Keywords** — Each team draws 4 Keyword Cards.
- **Keyword slots** — Insert one keyword in each of the 4 screen positions.
- **Secrecy** — Keep your keywords hidden from the opposing team.
- **Code deck** — Each team takes the matching color deck (black or white).
- **Note sheet** — Each team takes 1 Note Sheet and writes team name.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
- **Center setup** — Put Sand Timer, Interception Tokens, and Miscommunication Tokens in the middle.
- **Encryptors** — Choose an initial Encryptor for each team.
- **Turn flow** — White Team sequence is resolved first, then Black Team sequence.

#### Round-1 Special Rule
- **No interception in round 1** — Neither team attempts to intercept the other team’s code in round 1.
- **Own decoding still happens** — Each team still tries to decode its own Encryptor’s clues in round 1.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Draw codes** — Each Encryptor secretly draws 1 Code Card.
2. **Write clues** — Each Encryptor writes 3 clues in code order (line 1, line 2, line 3).
3. **White clues aloud** — White Encryptor reads all 3 clues; Black team records them.
4. **Both teams decode** — Both teams discuss and write White code attempt in first response column.
5. **Interception attempt** — Black team states interception attempt for White code.
6. **Own-team attempt** — White team states its own decode attempt.
7. **Reveal** — White Encryptor reveals code; assign tokens if earned.
8. **Repeat for Black** — Run the same clue/guess/reveal flow for Black team clues.
9. **Round end check** — Check win/loss conditions, return used code cards to decks, shuffle, rotate Encryptors.

#### Round-1 Sequence Exception
- **Interception skipped** — Step 5 interception attempts are skipped in round 1 for both teams.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Encryptor Clue Rules
- **Allowed formats** — Clues can be words, phrases, sentences, sounds, humming, dance, or mime.
- **Clue separation** — Clues must be clearly distinct as first, second, and third clues.
- **Meaning-only rule** — Clues must point to keyword meaning, not form.
- **Forbidden spelling clues** — No letter hints or letter-count hints.
- **Forbidden position clues** — No hints about keyword slot position on screen.
- **Forbidden pronunciation clues** — No soundalike tricks.
- **Public-info rule** — Clues must rely only on publicly available information.
- **No private references** — No private life signals, shared secrets, or personal-only references.
- **Spell on request** — If asked, Encryptor must spell clues.
- **No edits** — A spoken clue cannot be changed afterward.
- **No repeats** — Same clue cannot be used more than once in the same game.
- **No code readout** — Encryptor may not read the code digits aloud.
- **No keyword-as-clue** — Encryptor may not use the keyword itself or its translation as a clue.

#### Team Guessing Actions
- **Discussion** — Teams discuss which keyword number each clue points to.
- **Write attempt** — Teams write a 3-digit code in clue order.
- **Exact-match interception** — Interception succeeds only on exact numbers in exact order.
- **Miscommunication check** — Own team gains Miscommunication token if its own code guess is wrong.
- **Simultaneous outcomes** — One reveal can award Interception to one team and Miscommunication to the other.

#### Encryptor Behavior Restriction
- **No reaction rule** — Encryptor must not react during teammates’ discussion.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Persistent Information Leak
- **Fixed keywords** — Each team keeps the same 4 keywords for the whole game.
- **Public clues** — All clues are spoken aloud to both teams.
- **Deduction growth** — Every new clue improves opponent interception models over time.

#### Note Sheet Engine
- **Attempt column** — First column stores attempted 3-digit responses.
- **Correct column** — Second column stores revealed true code.
- **Keyword buckets** — Bottom section groups clues by keyword number for pattern tracking.

#### Optional Time Pressure
- **Timer trigger** — If one Encryptor finishes writing, flip sand timer.
- **Writing deadline** — Other Encryptor must finish before timer expires.
- **Incomplete risk** — Missing the timer can leave fewer than 3 complete clues.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Win/Loss Checks (End of Round)
- **Win condition** — Your team wins immediately at 2 Interception Tokens.
- **Loss condition** — Your team loses immediately at 2 Miscommunication Tokens.

#### Tiebreaker Trigger Cases
1. **Simultaneous win/loss** — A team ends a round with both 2 Interception and 2 Miscommunication.
2. **Double interception finish** — Both teams gain their second Interception in the same round.
3. **Double miscommunication finish** — Both teams gain their second Miscommunication in the same round.
4. **Round-8 timeout** — No team has won or lost by end of round 8.

#### Tiebreak Scoring
- **Token points** — Interception = +1 point, Miscommunication = -1 point.
- **Primary tiebreak winner** — Team with higher token-point total wins.
- **Secondary tiebreak** — If still tied, each team guesses opponent’s 4 keywords.
- **Keyword-guess winner** — Team with more correct keyword guesses wins.
- **Final tie** — If still tied, both teams share victory.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Round 1 to Round 2 Priorities
- **Build clue lanes** — Use clues that your team can decode reliably across multiple rounds.
- **Avoid over-exposure** — Keep first clues specific enough for teammates but not obvious to opponents.
- **Log everything** — Record opponent clues immediately and cleanly on the correct side of the sheet.
- **Start mapping early** — Build opponent keyword hypotheses from round 1 even though interception is disabled.

#### Encryptor Technique
- **Stable signal style** — Keep clue style consistent enough that your own team can map quickly.
- **Controlled abstraction** — Prefer clues with clear in-team meaning but broad external interpretation.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Too-obvious clues** — Highly specific clues let opponents lock your keyword mapping by round 2-3.
- **Too-vague clues** — Overly abstract clues cause your own team to misdecode and lose tokens.
- **Poor sheet discipline** — Bad note tracking destroys your interception accuracy later.
- **Accidental clue reuse** — Repeating clue terms breaks rules and reveals patterns.
- **Encryptor reactions** — Facial or vocal reactions during team talk leak illegal information.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Clarity vs Secrecy
- **Clarity side** — Improves own-team decode reliability this round.
- **Secrecy side** — Reduces opponent interception confidence in future rounds.

#### Decision 2: Safe Decode vs Aggressive Interception
- **Safe decode** — Minimizes self-inflicted Miscommunication risk.
- **Aggressive interception** — Presses for fast win at higher error risk.

#### Decision 3: Short-Term Win vs Long-Term Information Control
- **Short-term clueing** — Strong immediate clue can win now but reveal too much.
- **Long-term clueing** — Slightly less direct clue can preserve keyword ambiguity for later rounds.

metadata.notes: Written directly from provided Decrypto rulebook excerpt, including token scoring and round-order details.
